abstract: Summary Qubism is a library of design patterns that focus on data aggregations and OLAP-like “cube” data. Cubism is implemented in Scala and Spark, and leverages XUnit and YPath strings to slice-and-dice segments for aggregations. This allows rows to be exploded in a map phase, annotated in a shuffle/sort phase, and then aggregated in a reduce phase. Jerome will discuss how Qubism is used to handle large-scale NLP tasks behind Demandbase’s intent solution
event_title: [RSVP @ SFScala]Enhancing Spark's Power with ZIO,Qubism & NLP at Scale,Using Nix
title: "Qubism and NLP at Scale with Scala" by Jerome Banks
